ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall implement a closed-loop motor control chain from speed reference to voltage command using speed control, current control, and motor monitoring components.
InputInterfaces: SpdRef; SpdFbk; IdFbk; IqFbk; VdcBus; MtrT
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Top-level flow: read inputs -> monitor limits -> speed PI -> current PI -> voltage outputs

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall route SpdCtrl IqRef output to CurrCtrl IqRef input without scaling loss.
TimingOrTrigger: Every speed control update and consumed by current control
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Create connector check between SpdCtrl and CurrCtrl

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall route MtrMon IqMaxOut output to SpdCtrl IqMaxIn input to limit commanded q-axis current during warning or fault conditions.
TimingOrTrigger: Updated by MtrMon and consumed by SpdCtrl
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision block: if protection active then reduce or block IqRef

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall schedule CurrCtrl faster than SpdCtrl because the inner current loop shall react before the outer speed loop changes IqRef.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Timing note: current loop is inner loop

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall schedule fast electrical protection monitoring at 1 ms and thermal monitoring at 10 ms.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Separate fast protection path and slow thermal path

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall force torque-producing current request to a safe limited value when overcurrent, overtemperature, DC-link fault, or overspeed protection is active.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: fault active? yes -> IqMax=0 or derated; no -> nominal limit

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall provide explicit motor protection status to the system manager and fault manager.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision hierarchy for highest active fault code

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall initialize all controller internal states before enabling cyclic control runnables.
OutputInterfaces: IqRef; VdCmd; VqCmd; IqMaxOut; status outputs
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Start -> initialize states -> set safe outputs -> end

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall reset or freeze controller integrators during non-run operation modes to avoid unintended torque after mode transition.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: OpMod==Run? no -> reset/freeze integrator

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall use typed sender-receiver interfaces with defined units, ranges, and computation methods for all cross-component signals.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Precondition block: validate/scaling for each input

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall protect shared controller and monitor internal states using exclusive areas where states may be accessed by more than one runnable or event.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Enter exclusive area -> update state -> exit exclusive area

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall avoid dynamic memory allocation inside cyclic control and protection runnables.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Implementation constraint note

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall convert speed error into q-axis current reference using a PI control algorithm with ramping, deadband, saturation, and anti-windup.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Read speed -> ramp ref -> error -> deadband -> PI -> saturate -> anti-windup -> write IqRef

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall read actual motor speed from SpeedObserver and speed reference from AppMgr through explicit RTE sender-receiver ports.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Input block for SpdFbk and SpdRef

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall limit IqRef to the lower of configured controller output limit and IqMaxIn from MtrMon.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Saturation block using min(controller limit, IqMaxIn)

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall initialize speed PI integrator to zero and initialize ramped speed reference with current feedback speed to avoid startup torque step.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Startup flow for state initialization

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall set IqRef to zero and reset the integrator when operation mode leaves Run.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: OpMod != Run -> reset state and output zero

ArchitectureRequirement: SpdCtrl shall expose speed controller gains, speed error dead zone, ramp rate, and anti-windup limit as calibration parameters.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Calibration parameter table for MUD

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall convert d-axis and q-axis current errors into d-axis and q-axis voltage commands using PI control, decoupling feedforward, and voltage limiting.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Read currents -> errors -> PI d/q -> decoupling -> voltage limit -> write Vd/Vq

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall consume IqRef from SpdCtrl and IdRef from MTPA or zero-reference source.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Input merge block for IdRef/IqRef

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall output voltage commands to SVM/PWM using signed voltage command interfaces with defined scaling.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Output block to PWM/SVM

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall limit the voltage vector magnitude according to DC bus voltage and configured voltage limit before publishing voltage commands.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: voltage magnitude > limit? yes -> scale Vd/Vq

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall initialize d-axis and q-axis PI integrator states to zero and publish safe zero voltage commands at startup.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Startup safe output block

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall complete execution within its 100 us period budget and shall not exceed the allocated timing budget.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: No blocking calls, no dynamic memory, fixed computation path

ArchitectureRequirement: CurrCtrl shall expose d/q PI gains, electrical parameters, nominal DC voltage, and voltage limit as calibration parameters.
InputInterfaces: KpId; KiId; KpIq; KiIq; Ld; Lq; Rs; VdcNom; VoltLim
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Calibration parameter table for MUD

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall monitor phase-current-derived Id/Iq, DC bus voltage, speed, and motor temperature to determine motor protection status.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Monitor flow with separate electrical and thermal branches

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall detect overcurrent using the d/q current vector magnitude compared against calibrated overcurrent threshold.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: Id^2 + Iq^2 > IOcThd^2

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall detect DC bus overvoltage and undervoltage using calibrated thresholds and publish a fault status when thresholds are violated.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision blocks for Vdc > OV and Vdc < UV

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall detect overspeed using actual speed compared against a calibrated overspeed threshold.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: SpdMon > OsSpdThd

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall detect overtemperature with hysteresis to prevent status chattering.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: temp >= threshold -> OT active; temp <= threshold-hyst -> clear

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall derate IqMaxOut based on motor temperature and shall set IqMaxOut to zero for active hard faults.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Decision: hard fault? zero; warning/thermal? derate; else nominal

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall initialize protection states to non-fault default and publish nominal IqMaxOut at startup if input values are valid.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Startup monitor state initialization

ArchitectureRequirement: MtrMon shall expose thresholds for overcurrent, overtemperature, DC bus limits, overspeed, nominal Iq limit, and derating slope as calibration parameters.
InputInterfaces: IOcThd; TOtThd; TOtHyst; VdcOvThd; VdcUvThd; OsSpdThd; IqMaxNom; IqDerateSlope
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Calibration parameter table for MUD

ArchitectureRequirement: The architecture shall be verified by component integration tests covering nominal run, startup, mode transition, current limit, overcurrent, overtemperature, DC-link fault, and overspeed scenarios.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Create test scenario table from each architecture requirement

ArchitectureRequirement: Each architecture requirement shall be traceable to one or more MUD flowchart steps, runnable requirements, ports, calibration parameters, and verification cases.
MUD_Flowchart_Derivation_Hint: Use ArchReqID in CSV flowchart rows and test cases
